title: [BUG] bd-close.sh is missing but referenced in Makefile and rules
description:
scripts/bd-close.sh does not exist, but it is:
- Called by `make bd-close` in the Makefile
- Referenced by .claude/rules/beads-github.md as the canonical way to close GitHub-linked beads issues

## What to build
Create scripts/bd-close.sh that:
1. Accepts one or more beads issue IDs as positional args
2. Accepts optional --reason="explanation" flag
3. Checks if the beads issue description contains a GitHub issue URL (https://github.com/.../issues/<n>)
4. If yes: closes the GitHub issue via gh CLI with a comment, then closes the beads issue
5. If no: behaves exactly like `bd close <id>`
6. Hardened with proper error handling (set -euo pipefail, validate args, check gh auth)
7. Supports multiple issues in one invocation (loop over args)

title: Refactor security-sweep finding-categories to stack-adaptive architecture
description:
Replace the single bash-focused finding-categories.md with a universal base + per-stack reference files. The security-sweep skill installs into any project type, so detection patterns must adapt to the actual stack.

## Structure
security-sweep/references/
  finding-categories.md        -- universal: 7 category definitions, no stack examples
  stacks/node-typescript.md    -- JWT, prototype pollution, template injection, etc.
  stacks/python.md             -- subprocess shell=True, SQLAlchemy f-strings, pickle, etc.
  stacks/react-native.md       -- AsyncStorage secrets, deep links, WebView, permissions
  stacks/bash-scripts.md       -- shell injection, unquoted vars, credential validation
  stacks/generic.md            -- fallback: language-agnostic patterns

## SKILL.md changes
Step 1 becomes detect-then-load:
1. Probe project (package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod, .sh file density, etc.)
2. Load finding-categories.md for category framework
3. Load matching stack file(s) for detection patterns
4. Fall back to generic.md if stack unrecognized

Multiple stack files may apply (e.g. Python service + bash deployment scripts).

title: Add CLAUDE.md secure coding conventions section
description:
Add a new section to CLAUDE.md (this repo's project CLAUDE.md) covering secure coding requirements for agents working here:

- No hardcoded credentials — all secrets via .env or AWS Secrets Manager
- No secrets in log output or error messages
- Validate-before-use at system boundaries (user input, external APIs)
- Safe error messages (no stack traces or internal paths to untrusted callers)
- External service credentials sourced from .env (never committed)
- Threat model requirement: new integrations must have at least one STRIDE pass

This section should also be added to the CLAUDE.md template that install-to-project.sh writes to target projects (currently the script strips project-specific sections but keeps headers).

title: Add GitHub Actions CI workflows (.github/workflows/)
description:
Create the .github/ directory with two initial workflows.

**compat.yml** — basic compatibility/lint CI:
- Triggers on push and PR to main
- SHA-pinned actions (never use mutable tags like actions/checkout@v4 — always pin to full SHA)
- Runs shellcheck against all .sh files in scripts/
- Permissions: contents: read

**secret-scanning.yml** — Gitleaks credential scanning:
- Triggers on every push and PR
- Initially set to manual trigger only (workflow_dispatch) pending GITLEAKS_LICENSE setup
- Uses gitleaks action, SHA-pinned

Also create:
- .gitleaks.toml — allowlist for known false positives (.env.example patterns, placeholder strings, integration docs)
- docs/ci-security.md — SHA resolution procedure (how to get a SHA from a tag), Dependabot guidance, GITLEAKS_LICENSE setup steps, false-positive handling

## Why
SHA-pinned actions prevent supply-chain attacks via mutable tags. This is the minimum CI baseline.

title: Add 5 security rules to .claude/rules/
description:
Create five new always-on rules that will be distributed to target projects via install-to-project.sh.

1. **secret-hygiene.md** — prevents credential hardcoding; stack-specific env var table showing what belongs in .env vs where secrets are sourced
2. **security-review.md** — trigger table: loads secure-sdlc or ce:managing-pipelines at the right moments (code review, pre-merge, CI changes)
3. **threat-modeling.md** — adds STRIDE threat model step to the spec-first layer model; when to trigger, what to produce
4. **dependency-audit.md** — SHA-pin verification before deploys; Brewfile provenance guidance; what to check on npm/pip installs
5. **security-sweep.md** — trigger phrases for retrospective security analysis via security-sweep skill

After creating rules, also update install-to-project.sh step 1 (rules copy) so these new files are included — currently it copies all *.md from .claude/rules/ so this may be automatic, but verify.

## Why
Security rules should be active in all projects this toolkit provisions, not just enforced ad hoc.
close_reason: Implemented — see review notes below for what source files would improve content

title: Add secure-sdlc and security-sweep skills to plugins/kf
description:
Two new skills providing security tooling for agents working in this stack.

**secure-sdlc** — reference library for threat modeling, security requirements, pre-merge review, and incident response. Should include:
- Main SKILL.md with activation triggers
- references/threat-model.md — STRIDE template adapted for this stack
- references/security-requirements.md — EARS invariants
- references/pre-merge-checklist.md — runnable commands + human-review items
- references/incident-response.md — playbooks (AWS, Figma, Atlassian, git history)

**security-sweep** — retrospective security analysis of PRs/branches; triages findings and creates beads tasks. Should include:
- Main SKILL.md
- references/finding-categories.md — categories (CRED, AUTH, INJECT, EXPOSE, SUPPLY, SCOPE, CI) with bad/good examples
- references/triage-guide.md — P0/P1/P2 criteria with bd create commands

Place in plugins/kf/skills/secure-sdlc/ and plugins/kf/skills/security-sweep/. These are hand-authored (not generated), so edit directly in plugins/kf/skills/.

## Note
You will need the source SKILL.md files from the private repo. Request them from the user.
close_reason: Implemented — see review notes below for what source files would improve content

title: Epic: Toolkit improvements — skills, rules, scripts, CI
description:
Umbrella epic for adding features and hardening from the tooling-improvements.md analysis. Each sub-task is a discrete unit of work derived from comparing the other private repo's recent changes against what currently exists in this project.

## Background
temp/tooling-improvements.md describes changes made in another private repo over the past two weeks. This epic tracks the equivalent work for ki-dev-toolkit (formerly claude-config).

## Scope
- New skills: figma-to-spec, secure-sdlc, security-sweep, report-toolkit-issue, intake-report
- New rules: secret-hygiene, security-review, threat-modeling, dependency-audit, security-sweep trigger, toolkit-feedback, maintainer-intake
- New scripts: bd-close.sh (missing but already referenced)
- CI: GitHub Actions workflows (compat + gitleaks)
- Script hardening: Atlassian/Figma credential setup in install-to-project.sh + setup.sh
- Docs: CLAUDE.md secure coding section, design-skills.md Figma trigger, git.md Fixes #N auto-close
- Design discussion: release-please automation for kf plugin
notes:
## Session State — 2026-04-27
- Done: All sub-tasks complete except rename epic and release-please decision
- Closed this session: secure-sdlc + security-sweep skills (with source file review), 5 security rules, figma-to-spec skill, report-toolkit-issue, intake-report, toolkit-feedback rule, maintainer-intake rule, bd-close.sh, GitHub Actions CI, security finding-categories refactored to stack-adaptive architecture (node-typescript, python, react-native, bash-scripts, generic stacks)
- Plugin at v1.4.4
- Next action: close umbrella epic once rename (gzq) and release-please (5rq) are resolved

title: Rename project from claude-config to agent-dev-harness
description:
Rename the GitHub repo and update all internal references throughout the codebase. This affects plugin marketplace URLs, script references, docs, CLAUDE.md, README, and local git config.

## What needs to happen
1. (Manual, user) Rename GitHub repo from claude-config to ki-dev-toolkit
2. Update all in-repo references: scripts/install-to-project.sh, scripts/new-project.sh, CLAUDE.md, README.md, any docs/ references, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
3. Update local git remote URL to point to new repo name
4. Verify plugin marketplace install still works with new URL

## Why
Clearer name that reflects this is Kelly's personal dev toolkit, not a generic config repo. Also removes the ambiguity with the old agent-dev-plugins name.
notes:
## Session State — 2026-04-27
- Waiting on: manual GitHub repo rename kirvin/claude-config → kirvin/ki-dev-toolkit
- Once renamed: sweep all internal references (CLAUDE.md, scripts/install-to-project.sh, scripts/new-project.sh, README, docs, .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), update local git remote
- Note: report-toolkit-issue skill also has a hardcoded reference to kirvin/claude-config that needs updating after rename

title: Add Makefile with plugin-skills target
description: User wants a Makefile target that runs generate-plugin-skills with --bump, then commits and pushes the result. Single command to sync skills, bump version, and ship.

title: Add generate-plugin-skills script to sync .agents/skills into plugin
description: Currently plugins/kf/skills/ is maintained by hand. Skills installed via the claude skill manager land in .agents/skills/ but must be manually copied into the plugin with their references/ subdirectories. This creates drift (ui-animation is missing 8 reference files; ui-ux-pro-max is missing entirely). A generator script reading skills-lock.json as the manifest would make syncing reliable and repeatable: copy SKILL.md + references/ from .agents/skills/<name>/ into plugins/kf/skills/<name>/, skipping skills from anthropics/skills (official marketplace), and optionally auto-bumping the plugin patch version.

title: Evaluate LSP / code-nav tooling for install-to-project.sh
description:
## Goal
Reduce reliance on grep/file-reads and documentation that drifts out of sync by giving Claude Code semantic code navigation (go-to-definition, find-references, symbol search, diagnostics) that works directly against the live codebase.

## Research completed (2026-04-27)
Full analysis done. Options evaluated:

- **Serena** (oraios/serena) — most mature, but requires 4 hooks + Python/uv, context-drift issue confirmed worsening with Claude Code Apr 2026 updates
- **cclsp** (ktnyt/cclsp) — discontinued Feb 2026; get_diagnostics for TypeScript broken by design
- **Official Anthropic LSP plugins** — race condition in plugin loader closed as 'not planned' (#31468); broken for many users
- **Piebald community LSP plugins** — patches Claude Code binary; must re-apply after every CC update; fragile for automation

## Why deferred
No option has a clean 'add to install-to-project.sh and forget it' story right now. Every path has ongoing maintenance friction, binary patching, or unreliable tool selection behavior.

## When to revisit
Check whether any of these has changed:
1. Anthropic fixes or reopens LSP plugin loader race condition (#31468)
2. Serena's context-drift issue improves (Claude tool selection behavior, or Serena hooks)
3. A new option emerges that works passively without hook enforcement

## References
- Serena: https://github.com/oraios/serena
- Piebald LSP plugins: https://github.com/Piebald-AI/claude-code-lsps
- LSP enforcement kit: https://github.com/nesaminua/claude-code-lsp-enforcement-kit
- Anthropic issue #31468 (race condition, closed not planned)

title: Design discussion: release-please automation for kf plugin
description:
The other reference repo uses release-please manifest mode to automate plugin versioning via conventional commits. Evaluate whether to adopt the same pattern here.

## Current state
Version bumps are done manually via `make plugin-release version=x.y.z` or `make plugin-release` (auto-bumps patch).

## What release-please would add
- release-please-config.json + .release-please-manifest.json at repo root
- .github/workflows/release-please.yml triggered on push to main
- Automated changelog and version bump PRs based on conventional commit prefixes
- Independent versioning per plugin path (only needed if we ever add a second plugin)

## Questions to decide before implementing
1. Is the manual `make plugin-release` fast enough? It already works well.
2. Does conventional commit discipline exist (or need enforcing) to make release-please useful?
3. Single plugin = simpler setup; is manifest mode worth the overhead?

## Recommendation
Discuss with user before building. If manual process is sufficient, close this issue. If release-please is desired, file a follow-on implementation task.
notes:
## Session State — 2026-04-27
- No decision made yet — carrying into next session
- Context: current make plugin-release workflow is manual but works. release-please would add automated changelog + version bump PRs based on conventional commits. Single kf plugin = low complexity for manifest mode. Main question: is conventional commit discipline already in place?

title: Add optional Figma credential setup to setup.sh and install-to-project.sh
description:
Add Figma API token setup as an optional step in the installation scripts. Most projects won't use it, so it must be skippable.

## setup.sh additions
After the AWS Bedrock section, add an optional Figma step:
- Check if FIGMA_API_TOKEN is already set in .env — skip if so
- Prompt: 'Set up Figma integration? (optional — needed for figma-to-spec skill) [y/N]'
- If yes: prompt for token, write FIGMA_API_TOKEN=<value> to .env
- Include a brief note on where to get the token (Figma → Settings → Account → Personal access tokens)

## install-to-project.sh additions
After the AWS settings.local.json section, read FIGMA_API_TOKEN from source .env.local/.env and write it into the target project's settings.local.json env block — only if it's set. If not set, skip silently.

## docs/integrations/figma.md
Create a short guide:
- What the Figma API token enables (figma-to-spec skill)
- How to get a token (Settings → Account → Personal access tokens)
- Where it's stored locally (.env)
- Note that this is optional and most projects don't need it
close_reason: Implemented — Atlassian removed from scope, Figma made optional

title: Add toolkit-feedback and maintainer-intake rules
description:
Two companion rules for the report-toolkit-issue and intake-report skills.

**.claude/rules/toolkit-feedback.md**
Trigger phrases: 'report a bug', 'file a feature request', 'this skill is broken', 'suggest improvement to the toolkit'
Action: Load Skill(kf:report-toolkit-issue)

**.claude/rules/maintainer-intake.md**
Trigger phrases: user pastes an error log or bug report directly in conversation while in a project that has the toolkit installed
Action: Load Skill(kf:intake-report) before starting any fix

Both rules should be distributed to target projects via install-to-project.sh.

## Dependency
Depends on report-toolkit-issue and intake-report skills existing first (separate issue).

title: Add report-toolkit-issue and intake-report skills to plugins/kf
description:
Two maintainer-workflow skills for handling bug reports and feature requests.

**report-toolkit-issue** — lets developers file GitHub issues in the ki-dev-toolkit repo directly from conversation:
- Trigger: user says 'report a bug' or 'file a feature request' about the toolkit
- Behavior: collect title, description, reproduction steps; call gh issue create against the toolkit repo
- Place in plugins/kf/skills/report-toolkit-issue/SKILL.md

**intake-report** — logs developer-reported bugs into beads before starting a fix:
- Trigger: maintainer receives a bug report or error log directly in conversation
- Behavior: extract the bug details, create a beads issue with structured description (what failed, reproduction steps, expected vs actual), then proceed to fix
- Place in plugins/kf/skills/intake-report/SKILL.md

## Related rule work
After creating these skills, create two companion rules (tracked in a separate issue):
- .claude/rules/toolkit-feedback.md — triggers report-toolkit-issue
- .claude/rules/maintainer-intake.md — triggers intake-report

title: Add figma-to-spec skill and update design-skills.md trigger table
description:
New skill that converts a Figma design URL into a structured spec doc.

## Skill location
plugins/kf/skills/figma-to-spec/SKILL.md (hand-authored, not generated)

## Skill behavior
When activated with a Figma URL, the skill should:
1. Extract component/frame structure from the Figma share URL (via browser/MCP or by asking user to describe what they see)
2. Produce a structured spec doc following the spec-first layer model (requirements, acceptance criteria, UI notes)
3. Output a plan for how to implement the design

## Rule update required
.claude/rules/design-skills.md: add a new trigger row for Figma URLs:
'When working from a Figma URL → load figma-to-spec first, then frontend-design and design-taste-frontend'

## Note
If Playwright MCP is available, the skill can navigate to the Figma URL directly. Otherwise it prompts the user to describe the relevant frames. Document both paths in the SKILL.md.

title: Port diagnose-url.js Playwright browser diagnostic script
description: Add a standalone Node.js + Playwright script to scripts/debug/ that navigates to any URL and produces a structured diagnostic: console errors/warnings, failed network requests, HTTP 4xx/5xx responses, load time, and a screenshot. Runnable via 'node diagnose-url.js <url>' or 'make diagnose url=<url>'. Sourced from historical-maps-v2 tooling harvest 2026-04-17 (commit e03be0c). Steps: create scripts/debug/, copy diagnose-url.js and package.json, npm install + playwright install chromium, add diagnose Makefile target, update .gitignore.

title: Port Makefile help target with awk auto-documentation
description: Add a self-documenting help target as the default goal to the project Makefile. Uses an awk one-liner to read ## Comment annotations from targets and print a formatted two-column table. Sourced from historical-maps-v2 tooling harvest 2026-04-17. Steps: add .DEFAULT_GOAL := help, add help target with awk one-liner, annotate existing targets with ## descriptions, add help to .PHONY.

title: Port Playwright MCP server config for in-session browser debugging
description: Add @playwright/mcp MCP server to .claude/settings.json so Claude can drive a real browser during debugging sessions (navigate pages, read console, inspect network, take screenshots). Sourced from historical-maps-v2 tooling harvest 2026-04-17. Steps: add mcpServers block to .claude/settings.json with playwright entry using 'npx @playwright/mcp@latest'. No install required — npx pulls on demand. Requires Playwright browsers on host; run 'npx playwright install chromium' if not present.

title: Minor doc updates: git.md Fixes #N, design-skills.md Figma trigger
description:
Two small rule/doc updates:

1. **.claude/rules/git.md** — Add explanation of the `Fixes #N` auto-close behavior for GitHub-linked beads issues. Explain that when a commit message includes `Fixes #N`, GitHub automatically closes issue #N on merge. This works in conjunction with bd-close.sh for beads-linked issues. Add a brief example.

2. **.claude/rules/design-skills.md** — Add a Figma trigger row to the skill selection table:
   | Working from a Figma URL | figma-to-spec, frontend-design, design-taste-frontend |
   
   Also add activation instruction: 'When a Figma URL is present in the request, load figma-to-spec before any other design skill.'

These are small changes but complete the rule coverage for features being added in other issues.
